product: VMware Cloud Foundation SDDC Manager REST API 9.1

create_token_pair.wire_rule: the body carries exactly the username and password keys, nothing else
create_token_pair.failure: non-201 decodes the Error envelope; the raised exception must not contain the password

refresh_access_token.request_body: the refresh token id serialized as a bare JSON string, e.g. "<uuid>" including the quotes

success.body: the new access token as a JSON string

refresh_access_token.failure: 404 means the refresh token is unknown or revoked; recovery is impossible and a VcfAuthException is raised

invalidate_refresh_token.request_body: the refresh token id serialized as a bare JSON string

token_manager_rules.attach: every data request carries 'Authorization: Bearer <current access token>'
token_manager_rules.refresh_trigger: only a 401 answer triggers a refresh; 403 and other statuses surface as VcfApiException without any token traffic
token_manager_rules.refresh_once: after a refresh the original request is retried exactly once; a second 401 raises VcfAuthException
token_manager_rules.serialization: concurrent requests that hit 401 for the same access token produce exactly ONE refresh call on the wire; late arrivals notice the rotated token and reuse it instead of refreshing again
token_manager_rules.shutdown: close() sends DELETE /v1/tokens/refresh-token once (idempotent - a second close sends nothing) and afterwards any get() raises IllegalStateException
token_manager_rules.secrecy: no exception message and no injected-logger line ever contains the password, any access token value, or the refresh token id; the logger must still record that a refresh happened

error_envelope.client_rule: non-2xx answers raise VcfApiException carrying statusCode/errorCode/referenceToken; 401 raises the VcfAuthException subclass
